reasoning: Base rate of new coronavirus pandemics is very low (1 in the last century). The discovery of a bat coronavirus capable of human infection is a relatively common occurrence in virological surveillance (e.g., previous findings like SHC014 and WIV1) and rarely translates immediately into a zoonotic spillover. Even if spillover were to occur, it must establish sustained human-to-human transmission, spread globally, and merit a formal pandemic declaration by the WHO—all within roughly 10 months (by December 31, 2025). Such rapid progression without current evidence of an active human outbreak is highly improbable. The freeze value of 3.2% likely reflects longshot bias. Therefore, a floor estimate is appropriate.
